steps.title: Target accidental influence
steps.content: The student's claim is narrow: costume designers for modern productions of A Midsummer Night's Dream sometimes include contemporary design elements unintentionally. The supporting quotation must therefore show a designer who meant to follow the historical setting but later recognized that a modern style had entered the designs anyway.

steps.title: Reject intentional choices
steps.content:
• Choice A describes careful attention to accessories that communicate how a character fit into society at the time, so it does not show accidental modern influence.
  • Choice B describes deliberate flexibility to communicate an idea to the audience, which is intentional rather than unintentional.
  • Choice C argues that costumes should always reflect the correct historical period and criticizes mixing periods.

steps.title: Match the later realization
steps.content: Choice D describes costuming a production of A Midsummer Night's Dream in the 1980s while focusing on historically correct materials and techniques. The designer then says that, looking back, 1980s fashion clearly influenced the designs. That supports the idea of an unintentional contemporary element. The answer is D.
